title: Steel Tongue Drum - V5 issue readiness packet

readiness.fabrication_authority: not build-ready without measured prototype data
readiness.v5_status: partial; packet/explorer surfaces exist, but CAD/DXF authority, measured tuning logs, and MCP provenance remain future work
readiness.authority_note: The workbook and packet tables are planning authority for first prototype layout. They do not prove final slot geometry, tuning, heat/stress behavior, or safety until the real blank, kerf coupon, first-strike, tuning, mount, finish, and drift checks are logged.
readiness.measurement_note: Every target frequency, slot geometry, heat/stress, tuning, support, and finish claim remains evidence-bound until validation.csv is populated from an actual steel prototype.

v5_gap_register.next_action: Create parametric CAD from the design-table and family-spec values before claiming V5 CAD authority.
Export a reviewed DXF with slot geometry, root radius, datum, kerf, and stress-relief notes before any cut-ready claim.
Populate validation.csv with real first-strike, trim, mount, finish, and drift readings for every tongue.
Log actual cutter, heat affected zone or taper, burr condition, warp, and slot-root inspection from coupon and prototype work.
Create only after CAD source exists; record as derived_preview or concept_only, not dimensional authority.
Start MCP provenance log when MCP-authored V5 artifacts are created.

notes: This manifest intentionally preserves the current Wolfram source path, wolfram-starter.wl; the inspected stash row used an older steel-tongue-drum-starter.wl name.
This round promotes issue/explorer readiness without claiming complete V5, L3, measured, or build-ready status.
